contradiction_text: The deployment was rejected as invalid for Lambda, yet ECS service definitions were still mutated, so the deploy both failed and changed production state.
related_claim_texts: A CI race condition and a follow-on deploy attribution error pushed a non-existent container ID into Firetiger’s deployment state, creating the conditions for the ingest outage.
Terraform rejected the Lambda change as invalid and aborted the deployment, but ECS service definitions had already been mutated.
disclosure: The article must keep visible that the system did not fail cleanly; partial deploy failure left inconsistent state in place.

contradiction_text: Firetiger detected the incident quickly in machine terms, but human operators were still notified about 8 hours late.
related_claim_texts: Firetiger detected the incident quickly in machine terms but failed to reach human operators in time because alert notification paths were misconfigured.
A detection agent flagged the abnormality but initially believed the problem was isolated to GitHub webhook ingest and only attempted to notify human operators.
disclosure: The article must keep visible that monitoring existed, but the machine-to-human escalation path failed operationally.

contradiction_text: Early machine understanding framed the problem as GitHub webhook ingest, while the actual outage affected broader ingest traffic for most customers.
related_claim_texts: Firetiger detected the incident quickly in machine terms but failed to reach human operators in time because alert notification paths were misconfigured.
The ingest outage materialized when invalid ECS definitions prevented replacement tasks from starting, leading frontend ingest load balancers to return 503s.
disclosure: The article must keep visible that early detection was directionally useful but too narrow to describe the full blast radius.

contradiction_text: The invalid deployment state was introduced on February 27, but the visible outage did not materialize until March 1 when tasks restarted.
related_claim_texts: A CI race condition and a follow-on deploy attribution error pushed a non-existent container ID into Firetiger’s deployment state, creating the conditions for the ingest outage.
The ingest outage materialized when invalid ECS definitions prevented replacement tasks from starting, leading frontend ingest load balancers to return 503s.
disclosure: The article must keep visible that the control-plane corruption was latent before ordinary task replacement turned it into a user-visible outage.
